  CONSTANT_Utf8 = 1, CONSTANT_Integer = 3, CONSTANT_Float = 4, CONSTANT_Long = 5,
  CONSTANT_Double = 6, CONSTANT_Class = 7, CONSTANT_String = 8, CONSTANT_Fieldref = 9,
  CONSTANT_Methodref = 10, CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref = 11, CONSTANT_NameAndType = 12, CONSTANT_Class_resolved = 13,
  CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved = 14, CONSTANT_Methodref_resolved = 15, CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref_resolved = 2
}
enum  access_flags_t {
  ACC_PUBLIC = 0x0001, ACC_PRIVATE = 0x0002, ACC_PROTECTED = 0x0004, ACC_STATIC = 0x0008,
  ACC_FINAL = 0x0010, ACC_SYNCHRONIZED = 0x0020, ACC_SUPER = 0x0020, ACC_VOLATILE = 0x0040,
  ACC_TRANSIENT = 0x0080, ACC_NATIVE = 0x0100, ACC_INTERFACE = 0x0200, ACC_ABSTRACT = 0x0400,
  ACC_STRICT = 0x0800, ACC_ARRAY = 0x1000, ACC_HAS_FINALIZER = 0x2000, ACC_LINKED = 0x1000,
  ACC_MAIN = 0x2000
}

uint32_t cf_load_u4 (class_file_t *)
void cf_seek (class_file_t *, long, int)
long cf_tell (class_file_t *)

Detailed Description

Wrapper structure for class file access, this file also contains the definitions which can be found in a class-file

Definition in file classfile.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum access_flags_t

Represents the different access flags of a class, field or method

Enumerator:
ACC_PUBLIC 

Public access is allowed.

ACC_PRIVATE 

This field or method is private.

ACC_PROTECTED 

This field is protected.

ACC_STATIC 

This field or method is static.

ACC_FINAL 

This object or method cannot be modified.

ACC_SYNCHRONIZED 

This method is synchronized.

ACC_SUPER 

Alters the behaviour of INVOKESPECIAL.

ACC_VOLATILE 

This field cannot be cached.

ACC_TRANSIENT 

Not written or read by a POM.

ACC_NATIVE 

This method is native.

ACC_INTERFACE 

This class is an interface.

ACC_ABSTRACT 

This class is abstract.

ACC_STRICT 

This method is strict-fp.

ACC_ARRAY 

This class is an array.

ACC_HAS_FINALIZER 

This class has a finalizer.

ACC_LINKED 

This method has been linked.

ACC_MAIN 

This is the main() method.

Definition at line 97 of file classfile.h.

enum const_pool_type_t

Defines the type of a constant pool element. Note that some of the entries are not part of the Java VM spec but were specifically added by the Jelatine JVM. Those are not found in regular class files but will be used by its in-memory representation

Enumerator:
CONSTANT_Utf8 

Modified UTF8 string.

CONSTANT_Integer 

32-bit Integer constant

CONSTANT_Float 

Single-precision floating point constant.

CONSTANT_Long 

64-bit Integer constant

CONSTANT_Double 

Double-precision floating point constant.

CONSTANT_Class 

Represents a class or an interface.

CONSTANT_String 

Java string constant.

CONSTANT_Fieldref 

Represents a class' field.

CONSTANT_Methodref 

Represents a class' method.

CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref 

Represents an interface's method.

CONSTANT_NameAndType 

Name and type of a field or method.

CONSTANT_Class_resolved 

Represents a resolved class.

CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved 

Represents a resolved field.

CONSTANT_Methodref_resolved 

Represents a resolved method.

CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref_resolved 

Represents a resolved interface method.

Definition at line 73 of file classfile.h.

enum primitive_type_t

Represents the basic type of a field or element of a Java class

Enumerator:
PT_BYTE 

Field of type byte.

PT_BOOL 

Field of type bool.

PT_CHAR 

Field of type char.

PT_SHORT 

Field of type short.

PT_INT 

Field of type int.

PT_FLOAT 

Field of type float.

PT_LONG 

Field of type long.

PT_DOUBLE 

Field of type double.

PT_REFERENCE 

Generic reference field.

PT_VOID 

Used only for method descriptors!

Definition at line 52 of file classfile.h.

Function Documentation

void cf_close ( class_file_t *  cf  ) 

Closes a class file and disposes the relevant structure

Parameters:
cf A pointer to the class_file_t structure to be closed and disposed

Definition at line 243 of file classfile.c.

References class_file_t::file, gc_free(), class_file_t::jar, and class_file_t::plain.

Referenced by load_bytecode(), load_class(), and load_exception_handlers().

uint8_t cf_load_u1 ( class_file_t *  cf  ) 

Reads a single-byte value from the provided class-file, throws an exception if the end of the file has been already reached

Parameters:
cf A pointer to an open class-file
Returns:
A single-byte constant read from the class-file

Definition at line 263 of file classfile.c.

References c_throw, class_file_t::file, class_file_t::jar, JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, and class_file_t::plain.

Referenced by cp_create(), and load_bytecode().

uint16_t cf_load_u2 ( class_file_t *  cf  ) 

Reads a two-bytes value from the provided class-file, throws an exception if the end of the file has been already reached

Parameters:
cf A pointer to an open class-file
Returns:
A two-bytes constant read from the class-file

uint32_t cf_load_u4 ( class_file_t *  cf  ) 

Reads a four-bytes value from the provided class-file, throws an exception if the end of the file has been already reached

Parameters:
cf A pointer to an open class-file
Returns:
A four-bytes constant read from the class-file

class_file_t* cf_open ( const char *  name  ) 

Opens a class file with the specified class name (in the internal class format) looking in the classpath directory or JAR file, throws an exception if the class file cannot be found or opened.

Parameters:
name The class name

Definition at line 212 of file classfile.c.

References classpath_t::boot, c_throw, cf_open_with_classpath(), classpath_t::entries, JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, and classpath_t::user.

Referenced by load_bytecode(), load_class(), and load_exception_handlers().

void cf_seek ( class_file_t *  cf,
long  offset,
int  whence 
)

Seeks to the position specified by the offset parameter, throws an exception if the position is out of the file's range. This function's semantic is the same as fseek() but only the SEEK_SET and SEEK_CUR whence modes are supported

Parameters:
cf A pointer to an open class-file
offset The new position in the class-file
whence From where to start seeking

Definition at line 350 of file classfile.c.

Referenced by load_attribute_Code_attributes(), load_attributes(), load_bytecode(), load_exception_handlers(), load_field_attributes(), load_method_attribute_Code(), and load_method_attributes().

long cf_tell ( class_file_t *  cf  ) 

Returns the position inside the current class-file

Parameters:
cf A pointer to an open class-file
Returns:
An integer holding the current position inside the class-file
